name: Yemeni Arabic (Ṣanʿānī)
notes: Yemeni Arabic — Ṣanʿānī (North Yemeni highland) as reference variety, per Watson (2002). ق qaf → /ɡ/ (voiced velar stop): Watson p.11 and §2.2 p.20 — *q is not realized in any lexeme, even Standard/religious words: al-gurʔān 'the Qur'an'. ج jim → /dʒ/ (voiced palatoalveolar affricate): Watson p.11, §2.2 p.20. The /ɡ/-jim reflex belongs to CAIRENE and the LOWER-Yemeni Taʿizz/Hugariyyah dialects (Watson §2.1.6 p.16), NOT Ṣanʿānī — hence /dʒ/ primary, /ɡ/ listed as the Taʿizzī alternate. Interdentals ث /θ/, ذ /ð/, ظ /ðˤ/ ALL RETAINED (§2.1.4 pp.14–15; inventory Table 2.2 p.19; contrast with interdental-losing Cairene §2.3 p.20). UNIQUE: ض ḍād merges with ظ ẓāʾ → /ðˤ/ (voiced pharyngealized interdental fricative) — §2.2 p.20, one Classical phoneme lost. No k → /tʃ/ (unlike Gulf). South Yemen (Aden/Taʿizz) patterns closer to Gulf/Cairene in the jim reflex.

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic
sources.notes: Read (full open-access PDF, 322 pp.; printed pages). p.11: Ṣanʿānī reflex of qāf is a voiced velar /ɡ/, and of jīm a palatoalveolar affricate /dʒ/. §2.2 p.20: *q not realized in any lexeme (al-gurʔān); *ḍ and *ẓ merge to /ðˤ/. §2.1.6 p.16: the /ɡ/-jīm reflex is Cairene + Taʿizz/Hugariyyah, not Ṣanʿānī. §2.1.4 pp.14-15 + Table 2.2 p.19: interdental fricatives retained; §2.3 p.20 contrast with Cairene interdental loss.

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Sanaani Arabic.

stress.notes: Arabic stress is QUANTITY-SENSITIVE: it falls on a syllable because that syllable is heavy, not because of any orthographic ending, so no ending table can express it. The cascade (Ryding, A Reference Grammar of MSA, CUP 2005, §2.3; Watson, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic, OUP 2002, ch.3): a superheavy final syllable (CVːC/CVCC) takes the stress (kiˈtaːb); otherwise a heavy penult (CVː/CVC) does (muˈdarris); otherwise the antepenult (ˈmadrasa). max_onset=1 because Arabic onsets are obligatory and hold exactly one consonant — a medial cluster splits, closing the preceding syllable, and that boundary is what makes the penult of mudarris heavy. INPUT CONTRACT: weight is only recoverable from a complete transcription, so this requires diacritized input; an undiacritized skeleton has no vowels to weigh. The registered wikipron gold carries no stress marks, but the benchmark strips stress from both sides by default, so this costs no PER.
